components.name: dipotassium hydrogen phosphate (K2HPO4)
components.evidence: P. putida KT2440 strains were cultivated in 4-fold buffered (15.52 g l-1 K2HPO4 and 6.52 g l-1 NaH2PO4) MSM medium57.

components.name: sodium dihydrogen phosphate (NaH2PO4)
components.evidence: P. putida KT2440 strains were cultivated in 4-fold buffered (15.52 g l-1 K2HPO4 and 6.52 g l-1 NaH2PO4) MSM medium57.

components.name: ammonium sulfate ((NH4)2SO4)
components.evidence: Wierckx 2005 (ref 57): 'Mineral salts medium was prepared as described by Hartmans et al., using 37 mM phosphate buffer at pH 7.0.' The de Bont-group (HdB) MSM uses 2.0 g/L (NH4)2SO4 (=15.1 mM) as sole N source; value matches the target's prior curated concentration. Exact primary Hartmans amount could not be verbatim-fetched (paywalled Springer / no OA full text).

components.name: magnesium chloride (MgCl2.6H2O)
components.evidence: de Bont-group (HdB) MSM contains 0.1 g/L MgCl2.6H2O (=0.49 mM); value matches the target's prior curated concentration. Exact primary Hartmans amount not verbatim-fetchable (paywalled).

components.name: EDTA
components.concentration: 40 mg/L (10 mL/L of 4 g/L trace-solution stock)
components.evidence: Wierckx 2005 Trace Solution 1 (per liter): 4 g EDTA, 0.2 g ZnSO4.7H2O, 0.1 g CaCl2.2H2O, 1.5 g FeSO4.7H2O, 0.02 g Na2MoO4.2H2O, 0.2 g CuSO4.5H2O, 0.04 g CoCl2.6H2O, 0.1 g MnCl2.4H2O; trace solution dosed at 10 mL/L.

components.name: 1,6-hexamethylenediamine (HMDA)
components.evidence: MSM containing 15 mM of HMDA, Ahx or e-caprolactam as sole carbon source. ... supplemented with HMDA, Ahx or e-caprolactam as sole carbon and nitrogen sources.

components.name: O2 (aerobic gas phase)
components.evidence: Precultures ... in a Multitron shaker (Infors) at 30 C and 180 r.p.m. ... Main cultures were grown in 96-well plates ... at 30 C and 225 r.p.m. shaking speed (aerobic, obligately aerobic P. putida).

media.base_medium: Mineral salts medium (MSM), 4-fold phosphate-buffered, = Hartmans-de Bont (HdB) mineral medium as used by the de Bont/Wierckx group; host Pseudomonas putida KT2440. Here the phosphate buffer is 4-fold concentrated and HMDA is the sole carbon/nitrogen source.

sources_chased.what: Paper main-text Methods (phosphate buffer strength, carbon source, aeration)
Base MSM cited as ref 57 = Wierckx et al. 2005, AEM 71:8221 (PMC1317433)
Primary MSM definition Hartmans-de Bont 1992 (doi 10.1007/BF00174849) and Hartmans 1989 (PMC203180) for exact (NH4)2SO4/MgCl2 amounts
sources_chased.how: WebFetch to doi/Springer (paywalled, 302/303 to auth) and Europe PMC fullTextXML (404); amounts taken from the well-established HdB recipe matching the target's prior values

missing_before: dipotassium hydrogen phosphate (K2HPO4) - was only listed as uncovered
EDTA - was only listed as uncovered
1,6-hexamethylenediamine (carbon/N source) - was only listed as uncovered
concentrations for all trace metals (were null) now recovered from Trace Solution 1
media.notes: MSM = de Bont/Wierckx-group Hartmans-de Bont mineral medium; paper only spells out the 4-fold phosphate buffer (15.52 g/L K2HPO4 + 6.52 g/L NaH2PO4) and 15 mM HMDA, and cites ref 57 (Wierckx 2005) for the rest. Wierckx 2005 defines the trace-element mix ('Trace Solution 1', dosed 10 mL/L) whose eight salts + EDTA exactly match the target's metal set, confirming completeness. The base MSM per Wierckx defers to Hartmans (37 mM phosphate, pH 7.0), whose (NH4)2SO4 (2 g/L, 15.1 mM) and MgCl2.6H2O (0.1 g/L, 0.49 mM) primary amounts could not be verbatim-fetched (paywalled Springer / no OA full text) but match the standard HdB recipe and the target's prior curated values. Trace Solution 1 contains no boron or nickel (those appear only in Wierckx's feed-only 'Trace Solution 2'), so B/Ni are correctly absent.
